stem: A quadratic function h(t) models the height above the ground, in meters, of a projectile t seconds after it had been launched vertically. If y = h(t) is graphed in the ty-plane, which of the following represents the real-life meaning of the y-intercept of the graph?

answerOptions.content: The time at which the projectile reaches its maximum height
The time at which the projectile hits the ground

rationale:
Choice A is correct. The y-intercept of the graph occurs at t = 0, the instant the projectile is launched. The value of h at t = 0 is therefore the height of the projectile at launch — its initial height above the ground.

Choice B describes the vertex of the parabola, not the y-intercept. Choices C and D describe particular t-values, not the y-value at t = 0.
